mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, generating negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in greater than 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero during documented recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals a collapsing real estate investment cycle that drags down construction and durable goods demand, producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts drop below the threshold but equity returns remain positive in greater than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with decelerating real GDP growth create stagflationary pressure that compresses equity multiples and triggers negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to predict return direction or yields positive returns in greater than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable consumer prices indicates healthy capacity utilization without inflationary overheating, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative in greater than 60% of observations when both conditions are met, or the signal loses predictive power outside mid-cycle regimes.
